The system handled straightforward factual questions well, especially when the KG supported the answer. However, for more complex literature-analysis and synthesis tasks, some responses seemed less reliable. I cannot say with certainty that the answers were wrong, but some benchmark details and literature references felt insufficiently grounded.

The system generally showed a good understanding of the scientific domain and used relevant concepts, benchmarks, and terminology. The integration of KG helped connect related information and provide contextualized answers, but in some of the more complex research-oriented queries, certain claims felt somewhat uncertain or insufficiently verified, so I was not fully confident in all outputs.

The background explanations and the evidence graph helped make the answers easier to follow, especially for more complex research questions. The graph structure was useful for understanding how concepts, papers, and benchmarks were connected, and it helped reduce some of the complexity of the task. However, in some cases the additional context also made uncertain claims appear more authoritative than they may actually have been.

The combination of textual explanations and graph visualization made the information easier to understand and navigate. The keywords and relationships helped clarify how the answer was constructed and provided useful context. In some cases, however,  the underlying evidence was not fully clear, considering the "high confidence" of the answers.

The evidence graph and keywords helped me trace where parts of the answers were coming from, especially for factual queries. But again, for complex synthesis and literature-related tasks, it was sometimes difficult to determine whether connections shown in the graph represented verified evidence or semantic associations inferred by the system.

The interface and graph structure made it relatively clear that the system combines LLM-based generation with KG connections. Also, thanks to the initial examples, it was easier to understand that the system performs better on straightforward factual retrieval.

The graph visualization and contextual explanations helped make the reasoning process more transparent by showing relationships between concepts, papers, and benchmarks. While the full reasoning process was not always completely verifiable, the system still provided more interpretability than a black-box response.

Seeing the graph and intermediate connections increased my understanding of how the answers were generated and made the process feel more transparent. At the same time, some responses appeared more certain than the available evidence justified, so I would still independently verify information before using it in research.
